extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won from the breakaway after POGAČAR's early attack failed to shake VINGEGAARD, who marked every move to preserve 2:22 GC lead.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR attacked before the final climb with 193km remaining, forcing VINGEGAARD to chase without full team support early.
MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe bridged to POGAČAR forming dangerous duo, temporarily isolating VINGEGAARD from JUMBO domestiques except VAN AERT.
VINGEGAARD neutralized POGAČAR by refusing to contest final sprint, denying bonus seconds while preserving identical finishing time.
MEINTJES Louis jumped six GC places via breakaway inclusion, exploiting relaxed peloton on non-mountain stage.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: attacked extremely early (193km out) suggesting desperation but lacked firepower to sustain pressure on isolated VINGEGAARD
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: absorbed early surprise attack without full team support, never showed distress despite JUMBO riding full gas all day
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: timed attack as last man to jump from break, won stage despite acknowledging he mistimed stage 8 finish
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: neutral: rode entire day in break but lost sprint, missed best opportunity for stage win
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: positive: bridged solo to POGAČAR's long-range attack, created dangerous GC duo ahead of yellow jersey group
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: only JUMBO domestique positioned with VINGEGAARD during early POGAČAR attack, critical insurance when team caught off guard
rider_signals.MEINTJES Louis: positive: gained six GC places via break, tactical coup on non-mountain stage with relaxed peloton control
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: negative: cracked badly on final climb, dropped to 11th overall losing significant GC time
rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: negative: almost made breakaway but cracked badly, finished only 12th after extended effort
